sync.name: sync
sync.content:
{sh:load {iv}

ffmpeg -i $FILE_1 -an v.mp4
ffmpeg -i $FILE_1 a.wav

vd=$(ffprobe -i v.mp4 -select_streams v:0 -show_entries format=duration -v quiet -of csv="p=0")
ad=$(ffprobe -i a.wav -select_streams a:0 -show_entries format=duration -v quiet -of csv="p=0")
fr=$(ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=r_frame_rate -of default=nokey=1:noprint_wrappers=1 $FILE_1)

echo '-# Use "alt" as the first arg to do vice-versa
'
if [ "{arg:0}" = "alt" ]; then
    ffmpeg -i v.mp4 -stream_loop -1 -i a.wav -af "atempo=$ad/$vd" -map 0:v -map 1:a -t $vd ./output/whar.mp4

    rm -f v.mp4 a.wav

    diff=$(echo "scale=6; $vd-$ad" | bc)
    speed=$(echo "scale=6; $ad/$vd" | bc)

    echo "Video: $vd
Audio: $ad

Speed Used: $speed
Diff: $diff"
else 
    ffmpeg -i v.mp4 -stream_loop -1 -i a.wav -vf "setpts=1/($vd/$ad)*PTS",fps=$fr -map 0:v -map 1:a -t $vd ./output/whar.mp4

    rm -f v.mp4 a.wav

    diff=$(echo "scale=6; $vd-$ad" | bc)
    speed=$(echo "scale=6; $vd/$ad" | bc)

    echo "Video: $vd
Audio: $ad

Speed Used: $speed
Diff: $diff"
fi}

p1280ff3.name: p1280ff3
p1280ff3.content:
Tag of `.t p1280ff!2`, but advanced and better.
Use `.t sync+` or `.t sync+ alt` to make it synced!
If you wanna see the updatelog, try typing in `.t p1280ff!3updatelog`
{eval:{text:https://pastebin.com/raw/yT5nMc72}}
